title: manihot carthagenensis subsp. glaziovii
intro: Manihot carthagenensis subsp. glaziovii, also known as cassava or yuca, is a perennial shrub native to South America, characterized by its edible root and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Manihot carthagenensis subsp. glaziovii include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
